name: Amal
description: Pure indigo blue and ivory. The entire field is built from stacked triangles — mountain forms, arrow heads, negative space in equal measure. Bold and graphic. Zero pile; the geometry does all the work.
dimensions: 300 x 200 cm (9 ft 10 in × 6 ft 7 in)

name: Asma
description: A single saturated field of chartreuse yellow-green. The tone shifts slightly from top to bottom — brighter at one end, cooler at the other. Completely flat-woven. The color does all the work.
dimensions: 300 x 200 cm (9 ft 10 in × 6 ft 7 in)

name: Aziza
description: A vintage Boujaad from its native region. Every color came from a plant. The field is dense with small motifs — each one singular. The fringe has survived intact.
material: Virgin wool, natural and plant-dyed yarns, cotton warp

name: Basma
description: Sand-yellow ground with a barely-there lattice of intersecting lines. Understated to the point of disappearing — and that is its quality. Flat-woven with a refined, quiet presence.
dimensions: 300 x 200 cm (9 ft 10 in × 6 ft 7 in)

name: Bouchra
description: A grid of burgundy lines on ivory. Precise and architectural, warm at the same time.
dimensions: 305 x 203 cm (10 ft 0 in × 6 ft 8 in)

name: Chaima
description: A Mrirt from the Middle Atlas. Fine pile, vivid and dense — every color in one tightly woven field.
dimensions: Made to order — from 5 x 7 ft (152 x 213 cm), see sizes below

name: Fadwa
description: An abstract cubist portrait, hand-knotted in cobalt blue, cerulean, mustard yellow, and charcoal grey against cream — fractured, geometric facial planes in the unmistakable language of Picasso's Cubism, translated into wool. A composition that could hang in a gallery as easily as it could anchor a room. White knotted tassel fringe finishes each end.
dimensions: 287 × 207 cm (9 ft 5 in × 6 ft 9 in)

name: Fatima
description: A vintage Boujaad, likely from the 1980s. Natural dyes. The field is full without being busy. Time has softened the palette without losing it.
material: Virgin wool, natural dyes, cotton warp

name: Ghita
description: Three color forms — dark brown, mauve, pale rose — settle against an ivory ground. Moroccan warmth without nostalgia.
dimensions: Made to order — from 5 x 7 ft (152 x 213 cm), see sizes below

name: Halima
description: A hand-knotted contemporary Moroccan rug in ivory wool. Bold blush pink and terracotta shapes occupy the field in an asymmetric, painterly composition — each form distinct, the whole quietly striking.
dimensions: 300 x 200 cm (9 ft 10 in × 6 ft 7 in)

name: Hanan
description: Dusty violet, flat-woven with a precise diamond grid stitched into the field. The color ranges from deep purple at the top to pale mauve at the base. A large format piece with real presence.
dimensions: 300 x 200 cm (9 ft 10 in × 6 ft 7 in)

name: Hasna
description: A white ground interrupted by two large dark maroon abstract forms — one at the top, one anchoring the lower field. Spare, graphic and contemporary in feeling. The restraint is deliberate.
dimensions: 300 x 200 cm (9 ft 10 in × 6 ft 7 in)

name: Hayat
description: Natural undyed tones — camel, sand and warm brown — arranged in a quiet diamond lattice. Nothing added, nothing taken away. A Kilim that earns its place by being exactly what it is.
dimensions: 300 x 200 cm (9 ft 10 in × 6 ft 7 in)

name: Hind
description: Ivory ground with alternating bands of bold orange and warm grey. The geometry is crisp and flat — this is pure structure. Two photos show the full scale from different angles.
dimensions: 300 x 200 cm (9 ft 10 in × 6 ft 7 in)

name: Ikram
description: Two worlds in one rug: on the left, a bold black-and-white checkerboard field. On the right, a hand-drawn composition of rectangles and abstract forms built from pure line. The alternating black-and-white fringe echoes the graphic tension of the design. Ikram is for rooms that can hold a statement.

name: Ilham
description: A repeat of abstract Berber marks arranged in rows. Pattern from a distance, texture up close.
dimensions: Made to order — from 5 x 7 ft (152 x 213 cm), see sizes below

name: Inas
description: Vivid red and deep forest green meet in a repeating geometric check, articulated in ivory. Flat-woven with sharp, uncompromising color. One for those who want the rug to hold the room.
dimensions: 300 x 200 cm (9 ft 10 in × 6 ft 7 in)

name: Jamila
description: Amazigh symbols across an ivory ground — squares, triangles, anthropomorphic figures. Each mark is a character in a writing system older than the alphabet.
dimensions: Made to order — from 5 x 7 ft (152 x 213 cm), see sizes below

name: Jihane
description: White ground, one motif: a large indigo blue cross spanning the full width of the field. Graphic minimalism — the kind that only works when the weave is faultless and the proportions are exact.
dimensions: 300 x 200 cm (9 ft 10 in × 6 ft 7 in)

name: Widad
description: A field of pure saffron. No motif, no border. A color this saturated needs nothing else.
dimensions: Made to order — from 5 x 7 ft (152 x 213 cm), see sizes below

name: Karima
description: A large contemporary Moroccan rug woven in three tones: warm cream, sandy beige, and bold royal blue. Scattered blue rectangles fill the field in an artful arrangement — each block placed with intention, creating a pattern that feels both spontaneous and resolved. White knotted tassel fringe at both ends.
dimensions: 244 × 157 cm (8 ft 0 in × 5 ft 2 in)

name: Kenza
description: The classic Beni Ourain form, rendered with a precision that feels almost architectural. Dark teal lines cross an ivory ground in clean diagonals.
dimensions: Made to order — from 5 x 7 ft (152 x 213 cm), see sizes below

name: Khadija
description: Deep teal ground with a repeating diamond lattice picked out in ivory. Flat-woven with precision — every line is deliberate. A piece that works in the room and holds its quality over time.
dimensions: 300 x 200 cm (9 ft 10 in × 6 ft 7 in)

name: Lamia
description: A large Beni Ourain in sun-bleached burnt orange, with broad grey abstract stripes crossing the field. The pile is deep and irregular — left long, high and plush, the pattern emerging slowly across the loom.
dimensions: 300 x 200 cm (9 ft 10 in × 6 ft 7 in)

name: Latifa
description: All ivory, no color — the pattern comes entirely from depth. Latifa is a sculpted maze: geometric corridors carved through the pile run across the entire surface, catching light differently depending on the time of day and viewing angle. The hem carries a delicate black-and-white dash accent. The corner-flip reveals a natural linen flat-woven back. A rug that reveals itself slowly.
dimensions: 271 × 200 cm (8 ft 11 in × 6 ft 7 in)

name: Layla
description: A vintage piece. Warm golden amber set against deep navy in a flat-woven geometric field. The colors have softened with age into something even better than when they were new.
dimensions: 300 x 200 cm (9 ft 10 in × 6 ft 7 in)

name: Leila
description: Vivid emerald green and ivory. The field is covered in stacked triangle forms — similar in spirit to Amal but with green doing the work instead of indigo. Flat-woven, bold, immediate.
dimensions: 300 x 200 cm (9 ft 10 in × 6 ft 7 in)

name: Lina
description: An ivory ground with a repeating diamond lattice in a soft, tone-on-tone weave. Quiet and understated, letting the pile texture and diamond structure carry the design.
dimensions: 300 x 200 cm (9 ft 10 in × 6 ft 7 in)

name: Loubna
description: A deep charcoal field. The color absorbs light rather than reflecting it. Nothing is added.
dimensions: Made to order — from 5 x 7 ft (152 x 213 cm), see sizes below

name: Maha
description: A vintage Boujaad from its native region. The field is dense with bold Amazigh diamond motifs — red, teal, pink and gold meeting on ivory. The colors shift from one register to the next, never repeating. The fringe is long and intact.
dimensions: 300 x 200 cm (9 ft 10 in × 6 ft 7 in)

name: Majda
description: A dense abstract composition in ivory wool: blocks of turquoise, olive, blush pink, and mustard yellow distributed across the field, interrupted by bold black zigzag and curved lines. A turquoise checkerboard section anchors one side; small brown and dark accents add depth throughout. Every inch tells a different story.
dimensions: 286 × 194 cm (9 ft 5 in × 6 ft 4 in)

name: Malika
description: A Boujaad from the plains of Khouribga. Warm earth tones in natural wool — cotton, rust, and sand. Compact and characterful.
material: Recycled textile (cotton, jersey, synthetics) on cotton warp

name: Manal
description: Geometric blocks of color fill the field — orange, blue, teal and terracotta arranged without symmetry. The composition shifts mid-field, like two rugs stitched into one idea. The lower half opens into bold fringe.
dimensions: 300 x 200 cm (9 ft 10 in × 6 ft 7 in)

name: Meryem
description: Ivory ground with a central field of black motifs — towers, zigzags, abstract forms that suggest a skyline or a script.
dimensions: Made to order — from 5 x 7 ft (152 x 213 cm), see sizes below

name: Mouna
description: Deep red and ochre dominate the field, with flashes of teal and ivory cutting through the density. The motif work is continuous — no blank ground visible. A vintage Boujaad at its most saturated.
dimensions: 300 x 200 cm (9 ft 10 in × 6 ft 7 in)

name: Nabila
description: Woven in deep forest green, its field crossed by free-form black line art that suggests movement, figure, and landscape without committing to any of them. A bold black-and-white checkerboard border frames the composition, with small red accents punctuating the design. Contemporary Moroccan weaving at its most expressive.

name: Naima
description: Two scales of pattern share an ivory ground — large abstract forms and smaller repeated marks. The composition is asymmetric and deliberate.
dimensions: Made to order — from 5 x 7 ft (152 x 213 cm), see sizes below

name: Najat
description: An ivory ground crossed by a full diamond lattice in a spectrum of color — purple, blue, green, pink, orange. No two lozenges are the same. The structure is precise; the color is anything but restrained.
dimensions: 305 x 192 cm (10 ft 0 in × 6 ft 4 in)

name: Najoua
description: Pure cobalt and royal blue from end to end, with white lines that trace a series of rectangular frames across the field — a calm geometric grid floating on an ocean of color. The pile is full and plush, the white braided fringe punctuates each short end. The corner-flip reveals a flat-woven white-and-blue natural back. A Beni Ourain that takes blue as seriously as it can go.
dimensions: 245 x 160 cm (8 ft 0 in × 5 ft 3 in)

name: Nawal
description: A Mrirt runner woven in deep teal wool, its full field crossed by an all-over ivory diamond lattice. The high pile gives it weight and warmth; the color gives it presence. Nothing else competes.
dimensions: 300 x 200 cm (9 ft 10 in × 6 ft 7 in)

name: Nihal
description: Warm terracotta and burnt orange fill the field, scattered with crosses, zigzags and abstract Amazigh marks in green, teal and ivory. The border is a continuous zigzag. A rug that reads like a field journal from the Atlas.
dimensions: 300 x 200 cm (9 ft 10 in × 6 ft 7 in)

name: Nour
description: A bold Mrirt in ivory and deep indigo — a field of hand-knotted dots radiates out to a striped, nested-chevron border framing a central medallion. Graphic, symmetrical, and unmistakably tribal.
material: High-pile virgin wool, hand-knotted on wool warp

name: Noura
description: An ivory ground structured with a bold diamond lattice, each lozenge filled with a different color — purple, blue, green, pink, orange. The field is consistent in its grid but never monotonous. A generous size.
dimensions: 300 x 200 cm (9 ft 10 in × 6 ft 7 in)

name: Oumaima
description: A checkerboard of rose pink and ivory. The pattern is simple. The color is not.
dimensions: Made to order — from 5 x 7 ft (152 x 213 cm), see sizes below

name: Rachida
description: A classic Beni Ourain in natural undyed wool — pure ivory throughout, its wide diamond lattice barely distinguishable from the field. The high pile is exceptionally plush, the fringe loose and long. No dyes, no additions. This is Moroccan weaving in its most elemental form.
dimensions: 300 × 200 cm (9 ft 10 in × 6 ft 7 in)

name: Rahma
description: Wave-like stripe columns run the full length of the rug — each one a different color, overlapping and blurring at the edges. The effect is painterly and in motion. An unusually abstract composition for a Boujaad.
dimensions: 300 x 200 cm (9 ft 10 in × 6 ft 7 in)

name: Hassania
description: A grey ground with ivory shapes that read differently depending on where you stand. The weight of something contemporary made by hand.
dimensions: Made to order — from 5 x 7 ft (152 x 213 cm), see sizes below

name: Rim
description: An ivory field scattered with Amazigh marks — crosses, stars, stylised animal figures and abstract symbols in red, green and brown. The motifs are arranged without hierarchy, distributed freely across the ground. A rare figurative Boujaad.
dimensions: 300 x 200 cm (9 ft 10 in × 6 ft 7 in)

name: Saadia
description: A Beni Ourain with the classic diamond lattice rendered in natural undyed wool on a warm ivory ground. What distinguishes Saadia is her fringe: long braided tassels that run along both short ends, each braid finished with a small knot — an extra detail rarely seen in contemporary pieces.
dimensions: 315 × 190 cm (10 ft 4 in × 6 ft 3 in)

name: Sabah
description: One of the largest in this collection. A bold central diamond anchors a generous field of mixed-color blocks in red, teal, gold and ivory. The composition is confident and the scale generous. A vintage Boujaad at full expression.
dimensions: 300 x 200 cm (9 ft 10 in × 6 ft 7 in)

name: Salma
description: An ivory ground with a sage geometric laid over it — a traditional diamond form at large scale. Photographed in an olive grove.
dimensions: Made to order — from 5 x 7 ft (152 x 213 cm), see sizes below

name: Sirine
description: Four color fields, no hierarchy between them. A bold navy stripe down one side, a diagonal sweep of electric lime green, a warm taupe triangle, and a hot pink rectangle — all in high pile, each meeting the others at a clean geometric edge. White and navy dash fringe completes the composition. Sirine is the rug that sets the mood for everything else in the room.
dimensions: 248 × 160 cm (8 ft 2 in × 5 ft 3 in)

name: Souad
description: A large all-ivory Beni Ourain carved with a single nested motif - stepped chevrons converging at the centre on a plain ivory ground. No colour, no distraction: the pattern is pure relief, revealed by light and shadow.
dimensions: Made to order — from 5 x 7 ft (152 x 213 cm), see sizes below
note: SET ASIDE 2026-07-20: the May-30 photos WA0276-0281 are actually THREE different near-identical ivory nested-maze rugs mixed across the old Lalla + Souad entries. Photos cannot be reliably assigned. Hold until Abdel reshoots all three separately. Then split back into 3 entries with correct photos/dims/prices.

name: Tafat
description: A grey ground with concentric geometric lines, worn with age. A Mrirt-technique rug — carved plush pile giving the surface real texture and depth.
dimensions: 290 x 199 cm (9 ft 6 in × 6 ft 6 in)

name: Wafaa
description: An ivory field of extraordinary depth. No pattern, no motif — just wool, piled high enough to lose your hand in.
dimensions: Made to order — from 5 x 7 ft (152 x 213 cm), see sizes below

name: Wahiba
description: A large Boujaad with a vibrant tribal composition of diamond and eye motifs in red, turquoise, cobalt blue, green, yellow, and pink — the language of the High Atlas rendered in color. Hand-knotted pile, with the flat-woven foundation visible on the back. White knotted fringe at both ends.
dimensions: 307 × 186 cm (10 ft 1 in × 6 ft 1 in)

name: Yasmine
description: An ivory ground interrupted by a vertical composition in terracotta, black and undyed wool. Neither purely geometric nor figurative.
dimensions: Made to order — from 5 x 7 ft (152 x 213 cm), see sizes below

name: Zara
description: A Mrirt with a traditional medallion field. Earth tones and high pile, made for a room that is lived in. Middle Atlas workmanship.
material: Virgin wool, natural dyes, wool warp

name: Zeinab
description: A full-field Moroccan rug in rich, energetic multicolor. Geometric motifs cover every inch — diamonds, crosses, lozenges — with no single color dominant. Four photographs show the rug from every angle so nothing is hidden.
dimensions: 300 x 200 cm (9 ft 10 in × 6 ft 7 in)

name: Zineb
description: A grey field divided into irregular raised squares — the texture of old stone translated into wool. No dye, no added pattern. Just structure.
dimensions: 300 x 200 cm (9 ft 10 in × 6 ft 7 in)

name: Warda
description: An ivory ground crossed by loose vertical black lines, with solid blocks of mustard yellow, terracotta and dusty blue breaking the field. Abstract and architectural, with real warmth in the color choices.
dimensions: 241 x 163 cm (7 ft 11 in × 5 ft 4 in)

name: Samia
description: An ivory ground scattered with whimsical multicolor arrow motifs in green, blue, yellow, brown and pink, plus a chain of nested diamond medallions down the center. Playful and folk-art in feeling.
dimensions: 242 x 159 cm (7 ft 11 in × 5 ft 3 in)

name: Malak
description: A vivid lime-green shag pile crossed by a bold orange diamond lattice. An unusual, high-energy colorway on the classic deep-pile construction.
dimensions: 243 x 152 cm (7 ft 12 in × 5 ft 0 in)

name: Sanaa
description: A saturated chartreuse ground divided into a brick-like grid by black and white striped lines. Bold and graphic, medium pile.
dimensions: 253 x 148 cm (8 ft 4 in × 4 ft 10 in)

name: Assia
description: An all-ivory Beni Ourain where the pattern is pure carved depth. Sweeping chevrons and arcs are sculpted into the plush undyed pile around a central diamond, revealing themselves only as the light crosses the room. Finished with a hand-braided dark-flecked fringe.
dimensions: 284 x 212 cm (9 ft 4 in × 6 ft 11 in)

name: Kawtar
description: An ivory ground crossed by a black grid, with a solid red block and a black block anchoring two corners. A quiet nod to Mondrian, low pile.
dimensions: 296 x 212 cm (9 ft 8 in × 6 ft 11 in)

name: Rihab
description: An ivory ground with a diagonal chain of diamonds in olive, mustard, pink and blue, scattered with small colorful accent marks. Playful, high pile.
dimensions: 297 x 203 cm (9 ft 9 in × 6 ft 8 in)

name: Zahia
description: A dense field of tribal symbols — crosses, flags, small figures — in muted burgundy, grey and orange, framed by a vivid blue, green and red striped border.
dimensions: 298 x 211 cm (9 ft 9 in × 6 ft 11 in)
material: Virgin wool, natural and plant-dyed yarns, cotton warp

name: Ouafa
description: A teal and ivory concentric rectangle pattern, carved in relief from a single high pile. Graphic and hypnotic.
dimensions: 300 x 190 cm (9 ft 10 in × 6 ft 3 in)

name: Hafsa
description: A repeating diamond checkerboard in dusty pink, grey and purple across the full field. An unusual three-color take on the classic lattice.
dimensions: 300 x 220 cm (9 ft 10 in × 7 ft 3 in)

name: Sabrine
description: An ivory ground scattered with small confetti-like dots in neon pink, orange, blue, chartreuse and deep maroon. Minimal and playful.
dimensions: 306 x 199 cm (10 ft 0 in × 6 ft 6 in)

name: Itto
dimensions: 300 x 200 cm (9 ft 10 in × 6 ft 7 in)
description: Carved stepped channels interlock along a raised center ridge, like a city plan drawn in wool — the pattern is pure sculpture, revealed by light and shadow rather than color. High pile, hand-knotted fringe.

name: Tilila
dimensions: 314 x 216 cm (10 ft 4 in × 7 ft 1 in)
description: A warm grey field flecked with natural undyed tones, punctuated by groups of black tally-mark stripes that march down the rug like a woven score. Black end-stripes close the composition. Graphic and quiet at once.

name: Touda
dimensions: 315 x 196 cm (10 ft 4 in × 6 ft 5 in)
description: The archetypal Beni Ourain: warm ivory wool with the traditional diamond lattice drawn so softly it almost dissolves into the pile. The kind of rug that anchors a room without asking for attention. Hand-braided fringe.

name: Yenna
dimensions: 311 x 200 cm (10 ft 2 in × 6 ft 7 in)
description: An all-ivory Beni Ourain built from sculpted rectangular panels — the pattern lives entirely in the pile, carved blocks emerging and receding as the light moves across the room. No dye, no contrast: pure undyed wool and shadow. Hand-knotted fringe.

name: Yakout
description: A soft cream Beni Ourain with a gentle tone-on-tone diamond lattice, hand-knotted in undyed Atlas wool. Compact, plush and quietly classic — a warm neutral foundation for any room.
dimensions: 240 x 144 cm (7 ft 10 in × 4 ft 9 in)

name: Dassine
description: A pale, quietly textured Moroccan rug in undyed wool — subtle carved relief across an ivory field. Understated and calm, it lets the wool and the room breathe.
dimensions: 274 x 156 cm (9 ft 0 in × 5 ft 1 in)

name: Chama
description: A deep terracotta shag hand-knotted in high-pile wool, its surface carved into soft brick-like panels that catch the light — warm, sculptural and plush underfoot, a sunset-toned floor.
dimensions: 303 x 210 cm (9 ft 11 in × 6 ft 11 in)

name: Dihya
description: A bold contemporary color-block — clean fields of rust-orange and olive-green float on a cream ground of plush high-pile wool. Modern, graphic and completely one-of-a-kind.
dimensions: 305 x 215 cm (10 ft 0 in × 7 ft 1 in)

name: Tanirt
description: A serene ivory Beni Ourain — Tanirt means angel — with a whisper-soft tone-on-tone diamond lattice and deep, cloud-like pile. Timeless, calm and one of a kind.
dimensions: 309 x 200 cm (10 ft 2 in × 6 ft 7 in)

name: Tislit
description: A blush-pink Beni Mrirt with a bold abstract colour-block in chocolate and dusty rose, hand-knotted in thick Atlas wool. The high pile is deep and soft underfoot, and the composition reads like a modern painting — warm, graphic and completely one of a kind.
dimensions: 194 x 127 cm (6 ft 4 in × 4 ft 2 in)
